summary: Intervertebral bones are typically found within spinal vertebrae; however, for this specific case involving anatomic regions near a tooth’s roots ("interradicular"), these refer more likely to spongy/cancellous type as they surround and support each individual root tip area crucially involved during endodontic treatments such as apicoectomy surgery requiring careful navigation through these bony structures without damaging adjacent periodontal ligaments supporting healthy gums tissue attachment directly overlying those delicate canals containing nerves/pulp tissues needing preservation if possible throughout treatment planning stages focusing primarily upon maintaining optimal patient comfort levels post-procedurally while ensuring complete removal all infected material potentially causing pain otherwise left untreated leading ultimately successful healing outcomes minimizing risks associated complications arising later down line impacting overall long term oral
